# UX/UI Presentation Quality Checklist: Supply Chain Management DBMS

**Purpose**: Audit the completeness, clarity, consistency, and measurability of the user interface and presentation layer requirements.
**Created**: 2026-05-22
**Feature**: [spec.md](file:///C:/Users/thisf/Desktop/dbms%20project/specs/001-supply-chain-dbms/spec.md) | [plan.md](file:///C:/Users/thisf/Desktop/dbms%20project/specs/001-supply-chain-dbms/plan.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements. It validates requirements quality ("unit testing the English"), NOT the implementation code.

---

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are custom CSS design tokens (e.g., background gradients, glassmorphic blur, accent indigo, and cyan colors) fully specified? [Completeness, Plan §Technical Research]
- [ ] CHK002 Does the spec define visual loading and fallback states for the dashboard KPI widgets? [Completeness, Gap]
- [ ] CHK003 Are the visual structural requirements for the vertical Shipment Status Logs timeline fully detailed? [Completeness, Spec §User Story 3]
- [ ] CHK004 Does the spec specify the expected behavior of the Query Lab workbench when a query execution is in progress? [Completeness, Gap]
- [ ] CHK005 Are empty states (e.g., zero search results or empty warehouse inventory) visually specified with friendly indicators? [Completeness, Spec §Edge Cases]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK006 Is the "fully responsive" requirement quantified with explicit CSS media query breakpoint targets (e.g., mobile at 320px, desktop at 1440px)? [Clarity, Spec §Success Criteria]
- [ ] CHK007 Are the visual attributes of "low-stock alerts" (color coding, warning badges) clearly specified? [Clarity, Spec §User Story 1]
- [ ] CHK008 Is the layout structure of the master data listing tables (columns, sorting symbols, pagination size) explicitly defined? [Clarity, Spec §FR-007]
- [ ] CHK009 Is the visual presentation of "role constraints" (e.g. graying out buttons vs completely hiding them for Viewer) explicitly clarified? [Clarity, Spec §User Story 5]
- [ ] CHK010 Is "vibrant, premium feel" clarified with measurable styling standards or concrete UI component examples? [Ambiguity, Spec §Input]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK011 Are the styling properties for glassmorphism panels (background-blur, borders, shadows) consistent across the Dashboard, CRUD forms, and Query Lab? [Consistency, Plan §Technical Context]
- [ ] CHK012 Do form validation error displays maintain a consistent placement, color coding, and typography across all master data forms? [Consistency, Spec §FR-002]
- [ ] CHK013 Is the sidebar navigation layout and highlighted active link styling consistent between all Next.js page views? [Consistency, Plan §Project Structure]

## Scenario & Edge Case Coverage

- [ ] CHK014 Are the UI requirements specified for extreme data volumes, such as when the Query Lab executes a query returning thousands of rows? [Coverage, Gap]
- [ ] CHK015 Does the spec define the responsive layout wrapping rules for the CRUD modal form inputs when viewed on a small mobile device? [Coverage, Spec §Success Criteria]
- [ ] CHK016 Are requirements defined for displaying database connectivity errors or ODBC driver timeout messages to the end user in the UI? [Coverage, Gap]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK017 Can the "dashboard page load < 500ms" performance goal be objectively measured and verified in local execution? [Measurability, Plan §Technical Context]
- [ ] CHK018 Is the "smooth adaptation" on mobile devices defined with specific, testable visual layout constraints? [Measurability, Spec §Success Criteria]
- [ ] CHK019 Are the "user-friendly warnings" for invalid inputs defined with explicit copy examples and placement targets? [Measurability, Spec §User Story 2]

---

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline
- Link to relevant resources or documentation
- Items are numbered sequentially for easy reference
